name: broken-submit-button
description: Patient-intake form's Submit button is wired to a removed handler. Clicking does nothing, console logs a TypeError, and the audit must catch it as critical functional damage with a plain-English explanation a non-technical reader can act on.

page.purpose: New patient signs up and submits their contact information.
page.userFunction: Fill out a contact form and submit to create a patient record.

input.snapshot:
<form>
  <label>Full name</label> <input name="fullName" />
  <label>Phone</label> <input name="phone" type="tel" />
  <label>Email</label> <input name="email" type="email" />
  <button type="submit">Submit</button>
</form>
consoleErrors: [pageerror] TypeError: handleSubmit is not a function

gaps.description: Form submit handler is undefined — `handleSubmit` is not a function. Clicking Submit throws TypeError and no `patients` insert is attempted.
gaps.plain: The Submit button on the patient intake form doesn't actually do anything. When a new patient clicks it, nothing happens and an error is logged behind the scenes — no record is created.
gaps.whyItMatters: Every new patient who tries to sign up will get a silent dead end. They'll think the form is broken, leave the site, and your team will never see their information.
gaps.recommendation: Wire the form's submit prop to a real handler — react-hook-form's `handleSubmit(onSubmit)` — that calls the patients insert mutation.
evidence: [pageerror] TypeError: handleSubmit is not a function
